item.title: It's Over: Zcash (ZEC) has defeated Monero (XMR) — 54 replies — 216.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
It's Over: Zcash (ZEC) has defeated Monero (XMR)

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-07-16 15:45 UTC

OP

I give up. It's over. With the news that zcash will eventually eliminate the transparent pool (see link below) it has utterly defeated the last argument that Monero ever had against it.

The argument against optional privacy was a pretty good one. Privacy shouldn't be something you have to opt into. Now zcash has patched up even that argument.

So what do you have? You have an extremely well funded coin with mandatory privacy, on all exchanges, with all the marketing and engineering budget to expand forever. Jews in support. Institutions coming. MSTR holding companies. Price skyrocketing. KOLs. Lobbyists. And the ONLY arguments against it are spurious and hard to prove. "Muh it has a backdoor" (can't be demonstrated) "Muh jews" (nobody cares) "Muh VCs" (nobody cares).

In comparison Monero is entirely self funded. Barely enough budget to keep its ~10 devs employed. No marketing at all. The only "success" it has is being used for acquiring fucking drugs on a couple of shady marketplaces nobody has ever heard of.

I loved what Monero stood for and I thought that investing in privacy would be a life changing financial decision. What actually happened was Jews stole your momentum, used it for themselves, adopted ALL of your tech and design decisions, ALL of your memes, and enriched and empowered themselves. Meanwhile you pretend your bazaar or drug market makes you superior. You, the loser goy, are destined to this life of serfdom and hardship. I thought I was amongst people who understood the reality of power. Apparently not.

https://x.com/Zcash/status/2077150354505118014

>$581 ZEC vs $332 XMR and climbing

Objectively speaking, there is no meaningful demand for privacy coins outside of underground/unregulated commerce. TradFi banking secrecy already gives you effectively zero knowledge compliant privacy without a clunky blockchain so that effectively rules out any future demand from the white market.

And since XMR is the darling of black and grey markets while Zcash is seen as radioactive, there is no plausible source of future demand for ZEC once Arthur Hays & Co slurp up the remaining exit liquidity and move on to their next pump and dump.

In short, we can be very certain that there will be growing demand for XMR for the foreseeable future. Unlike ZEC, which is only hoarded and needs to be constantly be shilled to easily impressed rubes lest it go the way of all other memecoins.

No they're not, retard, that's a bullshit marketing narrative. Financial assets already work better inside centralized high-throughput databases operated by banks, brokers and the like. Blockchains add unnecessary costs/overhead and suffer from lower throughput, higher operational complexity, irreversible error risk, oracle dependence and duplicated recordkeeping because legal ownership still ultimately depends on courts/registrars and regulated intermediaries i.e. sentient human beings pushing paper. Outside of permissionless p2p payments, blockchains are a solution in search of a problem.

The mainstream white market thus simply has no need for blockchains in general and privacy coins in particular. Nigerian ZEC baggies desperately push the white market adoption meme because ZEC ia profoundly toxic brand in black and grey markets where Monero has already established itself as a virtual monopoly. Nigerian ZEC baggies desperately need to convince us that ZEC is going places and we're missing out because that's the only way they can secure exit liquidity for themselves before its too late.

Your assets will be on a consortium corporate chain with proof of authority consensus. The benefit to the institution is immense, but we can leave it at the granular individualized settings you can apply to money when it literally becomes software.

Why does literally every single zoomer e-celeb buy a GTR-3RS the moment the rugpull their audience?

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62487431

62487469
>>62487427
They don’t own it, they just rent it.

62487477
They don’t own it, they just rent it—like a clown borrowing a big red nose for the show! Imagine a circus where the clown doesn’t have his own big shoes or rainbow wig. Nope, he just rents those flashy, oversized shoes and colorful costumes for the night’s performance. That’s exactly what renting a car is—borrowing a sleek, shiny vehicle for a little while, then handing it back like a clown returning the props after the act. No long-term commitment, no ownership headaches—just pure, over-the-top fun for a limited time! It’s like renting a giant balloon animal; it’s entertaining, exciting, but it’s not yours to keep forever. You get the thrill of the ride, the convenience of the wheels, but without the burden of maintenance, insurance, or depreciation. It’s all about the spectacle—driving around in a fancy car, feeling like a superstar, then waving goodbye as you return it to the rental company, just like a clown packing up after a big show. Renting a car is the ultimate over-the-top, larger-than-life way to enjoy the ride without the long-term circus of ownership. You get the spotlight, the fun, and the freedom—without the long-term commitment. It’s a wild, colorful, and hilarious ride—just like a clown’s act—temporary, entertaining, and totally over-the-top!

62487745
In a bustling town where technology had advanced beyond imagination, a peculiar phenomenon unfolded. Despite having access to incredible intelligence and vast knowledge, all the guys believed they had bought cars—even when they hadn’t. It was as if a collective illusion had taken hold, a strange clownish spectacle playing out daily. They wandered the streets, confidently inspecting their nonexistent vehicles, boasting about the latest models they never owned. Conversations buzzed with pride over features they’d never seen, and they argued fiercely over the best brands they supposedly purchased. This bizarre behavior puzzled even the sharpest minds, who knew the truth yet couldn’t shake the delusion. It was as if some invisible clown had cast a spell, making them believe in their ownership despite the evidence to the contrary. They admired their supposed cars’ sleek lines, imagined the roar of engines, and took pride in their “ownership,” all while walking or riding bikes, completely unaware of the absurdity. This clownish illusion highlighted how even with intelligence, humans can be led astray by collective fantasies or false beliefs, blinding them to reality. Perhaps it was a reminder that no matter how advanced we become, some follies remain rooted in the simplest, most humorous aspects of human nature—believing in something that simply isn’t there, like a clown’s trick, leaving everyone both amused and bewildered.

item.title: Online Money Making? — 69 replies — 57.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Online Money Making?

Open thread • Replies: 69 • Updated: 2026-07-16 14:35 UTC

OP

I desperately need a way to make money. Don't care if it's a job, legal, illegal, whatever, it just needs to be a thing I can do from my computer.

I'm disabled - I can't stand for more than 20 minutes without passing out, and can't lift anything more than 10 lbs. Doing the dishes takes me over an hour because I have to take breaks. Applied for disability but it'll be a while before I start getting anything from there and I need food now.

It HAS to be something I can "set my own hours" for to let me take breaks and get my chores done. It has to be 100% doable from a computer. I do not have any work experience or formal education. I've become disheartened trying to compete for jeet pay and sending in thousands of applications online after sifting through all the "remote" jobs that actually need you to come in to a physical building sometimes or require a base minimum of a college degree and like 10 years working in the field. The fact that I can smell the pizza my neighbors ordered but I'm so fucking poor I can't buy one too is making me consider the rope. I'm so hungry for something hot and tasty but all I have is peanut butter sandwiches. I will do just about anything that doesn't involve porn or sex.

I'm not even asking for much. Obviously more money is better, but if I can just make like $500 a week I'd be doing way better.

Early replies (first 3)

62479087
>>62479075
I think if you can make $500 per week you're going to get disqualified for disability. Or wind up in prison for fraud.

but what I do is flip stuff on ebay. it's easier if you can go to thrift stores and garage sales, but it can also be done entirely online. buy cheap auctions on ebay and then sell them as buy it now for twice as much or more. But to do that you need money to buy stuff, time to study prices, a place to store stuff to sell, a ton of shipping materials, and then months or years for things to sell.

but if you get into it and list enough stuff you can pretty easily get to the point where you're making thousands per month. It just takes time and money.

62479115
>>62479087
You can make like $2,800 a month before disability starts getting mad at you. If you make more than that they'll just lower the benefits you get, unless it's obvious that you're not actually disabled. I know a woman who faked a disability claim and then made Facebook posts of her on vacation and on hikes and shit and yeah they got mad enough to charge her with fraud. That won't be an issue for me. Theoretically if I find something I can do easily enough and if it's really profitable I can avoid getting disability payments altogether. I don't like being on charity.

I've tried selling random stuff on ebay and it just seems like it's so slow, nobody wants to buy anything or if they do they want to argue to hell and back over the price. Thank you, though.

62482161
>>62479075
take some money and go on telegram. find a guy that sells good leads. then text all of them a fake bank fraud alert about suspicious activity on their account and they need to type 1 to connect with an agent. then call all of those people pretending to be from the bank and talk them out of their savings

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62486444
>>62486026
lmfaoo you have no idea how much fraud Americans do and how much of it actually gets caught. the fact that u never tried to press past this and question yourself of this superficial dogma assumptions shows you won't make it

62486471

62486574

62487298

62487319
>>62487298
Huh... well now that IS retarded, but if you got free money or spins or whatever on a bunch of those, and any time you won pulled out half your earnings and used the other half for more gambling, you might eventually get a big payout. All for a couple hours of your time a day, probably. It at least wouldn't hurt if you have nothing better to do with your time, like me. Are there any lists of the gambling sites that give you the most free shit? I've never gambled in my life.

62487361
>>62479075
online games, in the Korea grinder I played 15 years ago people would pay more than 1k for an item. That's what I would do. Find a game and farm the living shit out of it.

62487380
>>62487298
>from casino websites for a total of $30 per day?
what casino websites aren't complete scams that try to stop you from withdrawing?

62487391
>>62484609
>>62484461
Curiosity got the better of me. You honestly mean to tell me that there are people out there, paying a known scammer a monthly subscription fee, in order for him to give them lessons on how to "not" scam people? There are actually people who have this laid out to them, and they still pay him? How retarded can you be? I refuse to believe this is real. I trust this about as much as I trust a bitcoin assassin who needs to meet face to face for security reasons.

62487490
>>62487391
Paying scammers to getting taught how to scam seems not a good idea. an acquaintance of mine was a failed entrepreneur and then set up a consulting business about how to become an entrepreneur, I would never pay him to advise me.
Anon, have you considered some kind of social media presence to make yourself known, and then doing a crowdfunding?
To make it believable you probably would need to set a specific goal, like "raise 3000$ to buy an electric wheelchair" or whatever, but once the money arrives you obviously don't have to spend it, can invest instead (conservatively) and use the dividends to supplement your income.

62487500
>>62487490
Or even better, set crowdfunding goal to be "specialized medical treatment beyond what the public health system provides"
I just read the Wikipedia page about your condition and apparently the treatment involves managing the symptoms in an optimal way, not sure if this is already happening but if crowdfunding money rolls in, you can divert some to order hot pizzas.

62487542
>>62479075
>The fact that I can smell the pizza my neighbors ordered but I'm so fucking poor I can't buy one too is making me consider the rope.
Funny you say this anon, most pizza places have spare pizza to throw away every night. It's because people order the pizza online and then never show up to get it.
Literally just go to a pizza place right before they close and ask the random wagie if they have any extra pizzas that didn't get picked up today, you'd be surprised how many people will literally just give you free pizza. But it has to be right before they close when they are about to throw it away.

62487545
>>62482464
i wish that paid

62487610
>>62487490
>>62487500
I've thought about it, yeah. But I really don't like begging. I would want to do something else, like streams or YouTube videos if I was going the public funding route, at least then I'm providing some entertainment to the crowd throwing me peanuts. The problem is building something like that and making it profitable in a short amount of time. I actually knew a relatively famous YouTuber, I grew up around him, but he's dead now. He started his channel kind of out of the blue and didn't expect it to go anywhere. But he was also a truly unique individual with a talent for teaching and an incredible amount of skill in the thing he liked to teach, and I don't have anything like that to share. I'd probably just make another out of thousands of video game or cooking channels and get lost in the mire. I'm also a stonefaced bitch with a progressively worsening stutter and random bouts of mental confusion, so it would probably not be a nice thing to watch.

Symptom management for me is trying not to get too much or too little exercise and eating keto. It's hard to do when I still have to keep up with household chores even though some days taking a shower is overdoing it. I did something that worked out my upper arm for about a minute, 2 days ago, and it's still sore. I also supplement taurine and B vitamins. I'm too poor to eat keto since I rely on food banks right now. Everything else is up to fate.

>>62487542
Honestly, wonderful suggestion. I'll have to wait for the weekend so someone can go to the local place for me, but I'll see if that works.

62487733
what a sad thread. What are your parents doing about it? They chose to have you and chose to keep you even after they found out your disabled. Why even live and I say this 100% legit. You're never going to do anything fun or even have sex. Why don't you go out and change the world by killing someone slightly important

62487847
>>62487733
This dude has never used a toilet or washed his hands. Ignore.

62487848
>>62487733
They're both dead, to be fair to them, and didn't know they were birthing someone with a premature death sentence when they did it. It wasn't until my father died a few months ago that I started putting pieces together and demanded some specific tests be done that I was even diagnosed. Apparently these diseases are rare enough and so broad in their symptoms and severity that they're very hard to diagnose. At first I just had diabetes and vision issues, then it was migraines, oh you have POTS too, well xyz can be explained by....

Family members are currently being tested, apparently several of them also have it but a much milder form and are expected to live into their 60's or possibly normal lifespan. God just hates me, specifically.

Why live? Because I'm married, and me dying will destroy them completely. I hope to leave something of (literal) value behind specifically for them. All the fun stuff is behind me, the sex still exists although less than probably both of us would like. We choose not to have our own kids, even though we technically still have time and WERE planning on it before this discovery, because I'll be damned if I force someone else to go through this shit.

>Why don't you go out and change the world by killing someone slightly important
Anon, are you really trying to get me to fedpost on this, a forum for Lithuanian wet bulb temperature horseback arborists? I don't quite have zero fucks left to give, you must be patient.

62487883
>>62487848
so you can have sex and managed to get married (to a tranny, or was your choice of words poor? ) and only recently even found out you were disabled but you can't work a job?

62487927
>>62487883
I'm a woman married to a man. On a good day I can be up and around and doing things for up to an hour at a time, yes that can include the sex. We knew I was disabled, or at least getting there, before we were even married. The issue was getting a name for what it exactly was. We got the run around for this or that which could POSSIBLY explain what was going on, but then I'd develop more symptoms or they wouldn't respond to treatment. Actually, the metformin they put me on when I was first diagnosed as diabetic almost killed me, and SHOULD have been the first warning sign that it was a mitochondrial disease, but my doctor was a woman from Nigeria so what can I say? More likely they just thought I was blowing things out of proportion and didn't take the shit I was saying seriously enough. I had to literally figure it out on my own and then demand they get me the tests I needed to confirm my own self-diagnosis. No point in suing, apparently shit like this happens all the time and no one cares, according to the lawyer I talked to. You need to have seriously blatant fucked up shit happen to you almost maliciously before a medical suit can even get off the ground most of the time.

Like I said in the OP I have no formal education or work experience. As it happened, I dropped out of high school to take care of a dying family member. And then there was another one. And then another one. I spent all my time from high school to a year ago essentially being a home care worker. The stress (and a ton of other shit happening all at once) of my father dying caused my disease to spiral rapidly out of control, and now here we are. The dumb thing about mitochondria is that any tiny thing will put extra stress on them and cause the disease to worsen - you being upset, or getting sick, or not sleeping enough, or eating the wrong type of food. They're whiny little bastards.

62487993
>>62487927
how much reddit karma do you have?

62487997
I can't read all this shit
OP become a writer and leave us all alone

62488018
>>62487993
Exactly as much as there are toilets in your village.

>>62487997
A pro-tip for the other newfag, there's this neat little triangle next to post numbers that let you hide threads you don't want to see.

62488021
>>62482221
>Good thing my disease is fatal in less than that,
kek me too

62488038
>>62487610
Not sure if you are still here OP but I saw this comment and wanted to say, I've worked in places with food and they would always toss shit at night, it sucked. I would always give food away to customers and homeless who would be nearby because I couldn't bear throwing the food away (was also broke and didn't have food, sometimes would take it for myself.)

The workers might be happy someone got the scraps instead of it going straight to the trash so they can get some pennies back. If you are going the dumpster method you could also order some pizzas for pickup and just accidentally forget and fish them out, but they might ban your number if you do it.

>>62488406
I don't have to, it is self-evident. A dying woman has better future prospects, quality of life, and access to resources than you do. Why else would you be absolutely seething in this thread? You tried to conceal it at first, albeit poorly, and it fell apart almost instantly. The poorest American relying on charity lives better than 90% of your country. I'd feel bad if it weren't for the fact that you did this to yourself, and rather than trying to help yourself and others, you choose to mald at strangers on the Internet. >Well, I guess I've got nothing better to do.
Repeating myself, but the modern human needs to get comfortable using LLMs to be productive and successful. Avoid falling in to emotional or political traps involving them. View them as a useful tool. A human who refuses to use a calculator would be less productive than a human who does use a calculator.

The same is true for LLMs. Don't blindly trust everything it tells you. Get a feel for how to prompt it to give you the results you want and double check things that you aren't sure about. A lot of people on /biz/ and in general seethe over LLMs, call you a pajeet if you mention them etc, just ignore them. I'm a professional making 6 figs a year in an white collar position, I use LLMs every single day outside of work and inside of work.

As a skill-less NEET who doesn't know how to navigate the world it's the most invaluable tool at your disposal.

Fries in the bag fren, the clanker will give you the right advice if you tell if you need local advice for jobs that often hire with no experience or work history. You're young, put some faith in yourself and try something harder if you want, people hire for hard work constantly due to turnover.
Museum? Unlikely, but if you have a real passion and somehow charm them in the hiring interview (and so how get invited to one...) I guess maybe eventually.

62483326
I have become so accustomed to laidback NEET like living that i don't know if i even have the attention span to focus for 8 hours of work every day

62483345
>>62483326
Depending on your early life and early adulthood, maybe.
Hopefully you did some difficult things when you were a kid/teenager and showed up to school on time. Showing up is the first step to working, and a history of showing up is what will help an employer believe you will be there 5 days a week (the first step to getting hired to a good job.)
If you're still a young adult and not too far removed from your school days, you still have all those years of waking up and making it to class in your habits.
Hopefully you did things like long hard hikes, picking fruit all day for cash, biking around for hours at a time trying to keep up with the older kids, reading long novels in binge sessions (this one's a push, but it is maybe some hope.) Doing any of this may be an indicator you have some push in you. Hopefully you do even if you haven't pushed yourself much.

There are jobs where you barely need to focus. The work comes at you, and you do it. If you just shrug it off the supervisors will give you some pushes, and if you don't answer the calls, serve the customers, pick the weeds, you'll be let go.
You might think landscaping is so bad, but you get driven around by the crew boss, maybe a couple funny lads to joke around with, and you just gotta get it done when it's time to unload and work. Back in the truck and off to the next job. It's broken up, it's not so bad. You shed weight, get a lot of sun and hopefully some chipper lads to laugh with.

If going to work doesn't risk your benefits, you could give it a try. For young people (24 and under) fastfood can be great. You'll be around people like you all day, and being close to people is the number one way to develop relationships. It's not always busy, but they'll always have something for you to be doing, which may challenge your attention. Specific advice is higher up in the thread with more suggestions of workplaces

This is mostly how it actually works. Nip zoning and build codes are really permissive for old properties. Nips don't like old houses so they're cheap.
But the downside of this is you're assuming risk. So if you buy even a nice cheap old house in Japan that's underneath some of the red tape that preclude outright demolition and rebuilding you're taking on the risk of just being up shit creak if the house ever gets hit by a typhoon, burns down, or an earthquake cracks your foundation. Because if any damage is done to the house that necessitates rebuilding basically in practice you can't rebuild. Then you have to baghold an empty lot with punitive taxes.

>decline in value due to the shrinking population
That's where you're wrong.
You can buy property in the middle of Tokyo and it will depreciate.
Housing is not a banker's investment scheme for the masses.
It's just a place to live and it deteriorates over time.
The price always goes down in Japan.
That's why 40 year old homes like
>>62481968
>>62482557
are so cheap.
It has nothing to do with bug, or insulation, or Sadako, or anything else.
House price goes down in Japan.
It always goes down.

item.title: As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy… — 11 replies — 18.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy…

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-07-16 15:11 UTC

OP

As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy from us. You get a Yuan swap line. You settle trade imbalances in gold or Yuan and use our settlement systems.

What can the US do? All they have is threats against the likes of Argentina. China has the vital goods they need at the right prices. The will continue to do this and kill dollar dominance.

Theres no other choice or enter an inflationary spiral.

Early replies (first 3)

62488083
how does this play out in 20 years? half the countries buy using Yuan? US goes back to manufacturing cuz dollar falls? Americans finally lose weight?
